articles.title: Why Your Executives Need to Hear This Before Your Next AI Project
articles.summary: AI conversations are happening in most organisations right now, and if you work in data governance, you're probably right in the middle of it. AI is everywhere. Executives are excited, and rightly so. The possibilities are genuinely exciting: new services, reduced costs, greater efficiencies, things that simply weren't possible before. It's shiny, it's fast-moving, and there's enormous pressure to get on with it. And then there's you. The data governance professional. Quietly clearing your throat and saying, "But wait..." I get it. I really do. You’re not wrong to have concerns, but the way we

articles.title: Guest Blog from Helen Cullis - Why Having ‘Happy Data’ is the Key to Your Organisation’s Success
articles.summary: One of the things I've always found most valuable when explaining Data Governance is a good analogy. They help bridge that gap between abstract concepts and everyday understanding, making Data Governance feel less like technical jargon and more like common sense. Helen Cullis’s take on "happy data" does exactly this, and I think you're going to love her puppy analogy as much as I did when she first shared it with me. Over to Helen.. When I talk about happy data , I’m not trying to introduce anything revolutionary. This is really about a way of framing Data Governance that helps people across a

articles.title: Happy New Year - 2025 Roundup
articles.summary: Happy New Year and hello, 2026! I hope you’ve all had a wonderfully festive season and have emerged from your mince pie-induced comas ready to take 2026 by storm. As we go into the new year, it’s become a little tradition of mine to share my top 10 most popular blogs from the previous year. It’s always fascinating to see which topics resonated most with readers - sometimes it’s the posts I expected, other times it’s a surprise! Not only is it fascinating for me, but I think it’s quite helpful for readers to see which blogs have been hot topics. So, without further ado, here are the top 10 read

articles.title: The Tale of Dick Whittington and the Missing Data
articles.summary: Christmas is fast approaching, and it’s safe to say the season wouldn’t be complete without a few cheeky jokes, a whole lot of glitter, and an enthusiastic crowd shouting “It’s behind you!” at the top of their lungs. For those of you who have no idea what I’m talking about, a pantomime is a musical stage production which takes traditional fairy tales and retells them with larger-than-life characters, slapstick comedy, jokes, gags and a big baddie we can all boo at. They’re a staple of the British festive season, and, believe it or not, the perfect metaphor for data gone wrong. So, sit back, re

articles.title: Making Data Governance Processes Accessible
articles.summary: One of the most practical questions I encounter in data governance is deceptively simple: how should we document our processes? The answer reveals a crucial insight about implementation success, as formal documentation and user friendly communication require very different approaches. The Appeal of Swim Lane Diagrams My early career in banking introduced me to BPMN (Business Process Notation Modelling), which produces swim lane diagrams. These structured visual representations organise processes into logical rows, with each lane representing a specific role. The flow shows precisely who does w

articles.title: How AI Is Reinventing MDM and Data Governance
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is reshaping how organizations work with data, not just at the analytics layer, but much deeper in how data is defined, governed, and operationalized. While much of the attention is on new capabilities such as automation and generative insights, a more foundational shift is taking place. articles.title: From fragmented data to planetary-scale systems: why FSA/MEBS represents a step-change in enterprise modeling
articles.summary: For decades, organizations have been trying to model themselves clearly enough to build reliable, scalable, and future‑proof information systems. articles.title: Optimizing retail operations through a practical data strategy
articles.summary: Given the pace of change in the retail sector, impactful decisions can be a competitive advantage, but many organizations are still in the dark. They're not operating with actionable insights... trusting their gut to make decisions while keeping data in a silo. The solution? An all-inclusive data strategy that makes sense for the organization.This article [] The post Optimizing retail operations through a practical data strategy appeared first on LightsOnData .

articles.title: Transforming Marketing Data into Business Growth: Key Insights and Strategies
articles.summary: Marketing leaders and data professionals often grapple with a familiar challenge: how to transform marketing data into tangible business growth. During a recent episode of The Lights on Data Show, I had the privilege of speaking with Kasper Bossen-Rasmussen, founder and CEO of Accutics, about this very topic. articles.title: The future of generative AI’s form factor
articles.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, the form factor of generative AI is evolving rapidly. The concept of "form factor" encompasses the systems, interfaces, and user experiences that allow us to interact with AI. It's what bridges the gap between complex machine learning models and practical, everyday use cases. Webinar Audio - Navigating the Evolving Privacy Enforcement Landscape and AdTech: Practical Tips for Compliance
articles.summary: Listen to Meru Data's podcast on the rapidly evolving privacy enforcement landscape and AdTech compliance. Learn about the CPPA’s action against Tractor Supply, the new multi-state privacy consortium, and practical steps to strengthen your privacy program. Featuring insights from Priya Keshav and Sundeep Kapur, this IAPP-approved session offers clear guidance for navigating rising regulatory risks.

articles.title: Webinar Audio - Cross Border Privacy Rules (CBPR): A Comprehensive Overview
articles.summary: Listen to Meru Data’s podcast on the Cross Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) system — a global framework enabling secure, compliant cross-border data transfers. Experts from Meru Data, BBB National Programs, and OpenSpace discuss the evolution, benefits, and certification process of CBPR, and how it aligns with international privacy laws. Approved by IAPP for CPE credits.

articles.title: Webinar Audio - Conversation with Shannon Yavorsky and Priya Keshav.
articles.summary: In this episode, Priya Keshav and Shannon Yavorsky dive into the evolving world of AI governance and regulation. They discuss how organizations can design effective governance frameworks to align AI initiatives with core values, ensure transparency and accountability, and stay compliant with global regulations. Tune in to learn strategies that promote responsible AI adoption and help businesses turn compliance into a competitive advantage.

articles.title: Webinar Audio - Conversation with David Zetoony and Priya Keshav.
articles.summary: In this episode, Meru Data's Priya Keshav and privacy expert David A. Zetoony explore how to effectively conduct PIAs and DPIAs. They discuss best practices, regulatory requirements, risk mitigation, automation challenges, and tips for creating robust, defensible documentation. Whether you're new to privacy assessments or refining your current process, this conversation offers practical guidance to improve your approach.

articles.title: Navigating Privacy and other Risks: The Challenges of Agentic AI in Retail
articles.summary: Agentic AI is transforming retail with personalized shopping, smart inventory, and dynamic pricing—but it brings major challenges. From data privacy and cybersecurity risks to ethical concerns and system integration, retailers must address these issues to harness AI’s full potential responsibly.

articles.title: Securing Resilient Support for Your Information Governance Initiative (Part 2)
articles.summary: Selecting the right initial project(s), determining outcomes and measures, and preparing the business case are important groundwork for your Information Governance initiative, as discussed in Part 1. But to secure resilient management support for an ongoing initiative, youll also want to tie the individual projects to strategic objectives for Information Governance at your organization. articles.title: Securing Resilient Support for Your Information Governance Initiative (Part 1)
articles.summary: Management support is crucial for success with Information Governance initiatives. This is not merely a question of initial project and budget approvals. Most Information Governance initiatives involve behavioral changes in how data is handled, and in many instances, aspects of organizational culture may be impacted. No matter the ultimate benefits, any initiative involving behavioral change...

articles.title: Information Governance Day?
articles.summary: Apparently, today is Global information Governance Day. I frankly wasnt paying attention, because every day is information governance day here.
